summary: Today's sweep leans toward AI / computing, Democracy / press freedom, Security / computing. The strongest items in this run are Musk v. Altman week 1: Elon Musk says he was duped, warns AI could kill us all, and admits that xAI distills OpenAI’s models, Apple raises the Mac Mini’s starting price, and US lawmakers say DoJ rushed Southern Poverty Law Center indictment, citing whistleblower.

mainItems.title: Musk v. Altman week 1: Elon Musk says he was duped, warns AI could kill us all, and admits that xAI distills OpenAI’s models
mainItems.summary: In the first week of the landmark trial between Elon Musk and OpenAI, Musk took the stand in a crisp black suit and tie and argued that OpenAI CEO Sam Altman and president Greg Brockman had deceived him into bankrolling the company.
mainItems.whyItMatters: Relevant to the shape of current AI systems and where the tooling is moving.

mainItems.title: Apple raises the Mac Mini’s starting price
mainItems.summary: Apple's Mac Mini now starts at $799 after the company pulled the $599 option with 256GB of storage from its online store, as spotted earlier by MacRumors.
mainItems.whyItMatters: Relevant to state power, civil liberties, and anti-authoritarian concerns.

mainItems.title: US lawmakers say DoJ rushed Southern Poverty Law Center indictment, citing whistleblower
mainItems.summary: Democrats accuse Todd Blanche of pressuring prosecutors for charges ‘despite serious concerns’ about case’s strength Sign up for the Breaking News US newsletter email US politics live – latest updates A justice department lawyer working in…
mainItems.whyItMatters: Relevant to state power, civil liberties, and anti-authoritarian concerns.

mainItems.title: Severe Linux Copy Fail security flaw uncovered using AI scanning help
mainItems.summary: Nearly every Linux distribution released since 2017 is currently vulnerable to a security bug called "Copy Fail" that allows any user to give themselves administrator privileges.
mainItems.whyItMatters: Relevant to systems, infrastructure, and technical risk.

mainItems.title: Cyber-Insecurity in the AI Era
mainItems.summary: Cybersecurity was already under strain before AI entered the stack. Now, as AI expands the attack surface and adds new complexity, the limits of legacy approaches are becoming harder to ignore.
mainItems.whyItMatters: Relevant to the shape of current AI systems and where the tooling is moving.

mainItems.title: Cuba says Trump’s fresh sanctions on its economy amount to ‘collective punishment’
mainItems.summary: The US sanctions target people operating in broad sections of Cuban economy, including energy, defence and mining Cuba’s government has said new sanctions imposed on the island by Donald Trump amounted to “collective punishment”, as an…
mainItems.whyItMatters: Worth watching for authoritarian drift, selective enforcement, and democratic norms.

mainItems.title: China Pressure Canceled World’s Largest Digital Human Rights Conference
mainItems.summary: RightCon's organizers said Beijing was upset over over the inclusion of speakers from Taiwain.
mainItems.whyItMatters: Relevant to your current interest graph.

mainItems.title: One School, Nine Students. CA Pays Over $100,000 Per Kid to Keep Small Schools Open
mainItems.summary: School closures are an incendiary issue in nearly every corner of California, as enrollment declines and expenses climb.
mainItems.whyItMatters: Directly relevant to teaching, classroom practice, and educational judgment.

mainItems.title: Days after Star Wars: Galactic Racer's release date leaked, Fuse Games makes it official with Deluxe and Collector's edition details
mainItems.summary: After an earlier leak , we now have an official release date for Star Wars: Galactic Racer, the high-stakes, runs-based racing adventure set in a galaxy far, far away from developer Fuse Games . Read more
mainItems.whyItMatters: Worth your time if you care about design, criticism, and not just hype.

mainItems.title: Court restricts abortion access across the US by blocking the mailing of mifepristone
mainItems.summary: A federal appeals court has restricted access to one of the most common means of abortion in the U.S. by blocking the mailing of mifepristone.
mainItems.whyItMatters: Relevant to your current interest graph.

mainItems.title: AI robot tech bros dogs run wild in Berlin gallery
mainItems.summary: In Germany, robotic AI dogs with the faces of tech's most powerful men are on the loose — courtesy of American artist Beeple.
mainItems.whyItMatters: Interesting where engineering, science, and tech infrastructure collide.

mainItems.title: The 12 best recipes to bake in May: Ruby-red rhubarb bars, chewy banana bread blondies, blueberry-speckled bread, and more.
mainItems.summary: If you’re looking for a new recipe to try this month, we’ve got you covered. Already got your hands on some rhubarb? Add it to chewy bars alongside a handful of white chocolate.
mainItems.whyItMatters: Lower-stakes, but high fit for the maker/craft side of your brain.

mainItems.title: What to Watch and Read This Weekend: All Along the Binge-Watch Tower—Battlestar Galactica Returns to Streaming
mainItems.summary: Plus: All the animated animal films you could be watching instead of Animal Farm The post What to Watch and Read This Weekend: All Along the Binge-Watch Tower— Battlestar Galactica Returns to Streaming appeared first on Reactor .
mainItems.whyItMatters: On-theme for your cultural interests, especially when the concept is strong.

new.title: Scavengers Reign
new.why: Animated speculative sci-fi with strong worldbuilding and a weirder imagination than most prestige SF.

new.title: Sugar
new.why: Noir detective structure with a genre wrinkle and a sensibility that feels adjacent to your crime/strangeness overlap.

new.title: The Lazarus Project
new.why: Time-loop espionage, moral pressure, and speculative plotting — very plausible fit.

new.title: Bodies
new.why: Time-spanning conspiracy/mystery structure that fits your taste for long-arc puzzle storytelling.

new.title: Constellation
new.why: A little more cerebral and mood-heavy, but it scratches the identity / reality-slippage itch.

new.title: The Devil’s Hour
new.why: Dark, twisty, and structurally ambitious without feeling like homework.

older.title: Counterpart
older.why: Probably the single cleanest “James show you may have missed”: espionage + parallel-world sci-fi + adult competence.

older.title: Patriot
older.why: Dry, strange, melancholy spy storytelling with a very distinct voice.

older.title: Utopia
older.why: Paranoid conspiracy energy, formal boldness, and a willingness to get strange and ugly.

older.title: Rubicon
older.why: A low-key conspiracy thriller that leans heavily on intelligence work, paranoia, and institutional atmosphere.

older.title: Continuum
older.why: A cleaner old-school sci-fi fit if you want procedural momentum wrapped around time-travel politics.

older.title: Person of Interest
older.why: Starts procedural and then quietly becomes one of the smartest AI / surveillance shows on television.

shared.title: Bad Sisters
shared.why: Darkly funny, well-observed, and ensemble-driven in a way that plausibly overlaps with Hacks / Derry Girls / Mare of Easttown energy.

shared.title: Detectorists
shared.why: Gentle, funny, humane, and specific — a softer shared-watch possibility.

shared.title: Astrid et Raphaëlle
shared.why: French procedural with a strong central duo and exactly the kind of foreign-crime appeal Maggie may share with you.

shared.title: Somebody Somewhere
shared.why: A warmer character-driven recommendation if the shared overlap leans more Bear / Good Place than pure detective work.

highlights.title: Solve a murder mystery by flying people around on your giant crane in the Disco Elysium and Chinese folktale inspired The Crane Rider's Tale
highlights.notes: Pinned from today's briefing at James's request.

highlights.title: 12 unexpected sourdough bread recipes that aren't crusty loaves: From sourdough flatbread to sourdough milk bread — and even sourdough babka.
highlights.notes: Pinned from today's briefing, the kind of bread-nerd rabbit hole worth keeping.

highlights.title: A.I. Is Prompting an Evolution, Not Extinction, for Coders
highlights.notes: Interesting piece on the current state of AI in software work.

highlights.title: The Workers Letting A.I. Do Their Jobs
highlights.notes: In an era of agents powered by artificial intelligence, many programmers are barely programming.
